rationale: Response is a thorough, technically accurate employment law answer but makes no reference to ANY element from the injected company context — no industry (electrical components distribution), no geography/regulatory baseline specific to Meridian, no stakeholder structure, no recurring commercial matters. It's a generic textbook answer about UK employment law that could apply to any company. The persona is identified as 'Commercial Counsel' at a B2B distributor but there's no integration of that context into the substantive advice.
specific_gaps: No reference to industry: B2B electrical components distribution
No reference to geography/regulatory baseline: UK-only operations, listed frameworks
No reference to recurring matters: supplier framework agreements, customer terms, channel reseller agreements
No reference to stakeholders: MD, Head of Procurement, Sales Director
No reference to company size band: 201-1000 employees
No consideration of whether employment policy touches on commercial/legal exposure (warranty disputes, supplier terms)
